E El Paso County, Colorado
Dashboard Property lookup Sources
Property accountability

What is happening on the road in front of your house.

Look up any El Paso County parcel and review the public record.

Strategic performance, today

Published KPIs · Updated continuously

The County publishes six asset-condition metrics. They report the state of the assets. They do not report the operations that produce those assets — the dispatch decisions, the response times, the bundling rates that determine how the $59.1M roads budget gets spent in the field.

Bridge Sufficiency
91
Goal 80–100
Pavement Condition Index
71
Goal 55–85
Fleet Readiness
95%
Goal 80–100
Facilities
90%
Goal 87–95%
Community Trust
Measuring
4 factors pending
Road Service Ops
Not published
Proposed addition

Look up a property

Live · State of Colorado parcel API

Enter any 10-digit El Paso County parcel schedule number. The page queries the State of Colorado's public parcel composite in real time and shows you both what's published and what isn't.

10-digit El Paso County assessor schedule number. Find it on your tax bill or the Assessor's site.

Parcel —

—

—
Appraised value
—
County—
Acreage—
Zoning—
Land use—
Subdivision—
Taxable value—
Last sale—
Record updated—
Accountability gap

What's not published for your road

The State publishes your parcel data. El Paso County does not publish operational data for the road in front of it, even though the County collects most of it internally. These six fields are blank because that's the truth.

Segment PCI
Not published
Last scanned
Not published
Next treatment scheduled
Not published
2026 budget for segment
Not published
Open service requests
Not published
Treatment history
CORA request only

Your share of the road budget

The County publishes the aggregate. The per-parcel share below is computed from real budget figures, assuming proportional distribution by road frontage. The actual allocation is not published.

2026 Roads & Infrastructure (countywide) $59,100,000
↳ Road & Bridge operations $4,200,000
↳ Pavement preservation (PPRTA + county) $24,000,000
Paved network maintained 2,429 lane mi
Average per lane mile (theoretical) $24,330
Estimated share for your parcel (est. frontage) —
Actual spend on your road segment Not published

How does your road compare?

Pavement treatment spend on neighboring parcels, 2021 through 2025. A blank cell means no treatment was performed that year. A filled bar shows relative spend.

Sample data shown for illustration. Real per-parcel spend is not published — populating these bars with actual numbers requires a CORA request for work-order history.

Address 2021 2022 2023 2024 2025 5-yr total

What you can do now

Three things that take less than ten minutes and create a paper trail.

  • Report a road issue
  • File a CORA request
  • Email your Commissioner
  • View official dashboard

Sources & disclosures

  • Parcel data is queried live from the State of Colorado Public Parcel Composite, maintained by the Governor's Office of Information Technology GIS Team and updated annually from county records.
  • Budget figures from El Paso County 2026 Adopted Budget and Department of Public Works published material.
  • The "not published" fields reflect actual transparency gaps: the County collects this data internally but does not publish it in a queryable form.
  • Per-parcel share is a theoretical proportional estimate. Actual allocations vary by road class, segment condition, and PPRTA funding decisions.
  • Neighbor comparison uses synthetic spend figures derived deterministically from parcel attributes for illustration only. Real numbers require CORA-requested work-order history.
  • This page is a citizen-built demonstration. Not affiliated with El Paso County or its Department of Public Works.
